resources.string: Which keyword declares a read-only variable in Kotlin?
val declares a read-only reference that cannot be reassigned.
Which keyword declares a mutable variable in Kotlin?
var declares a variable whose value can change.
How do you declare a function in Kotlin?
In Kotlin, functions are declared with the fun keyword.
Which type represents text in Kotlin?
Which type represents true or false?
Boolean stores true or false values.
Which operator is used to check structural equality?
Which operator checks whether two references point to the same object?
Which symbol is used to allow null in a type?
The ? symbol means that a type can contain null, for example String?.
Which operator lets you access a property only if the object is not null?
The ?. operator is the safe call operator.
Which operator throws an exception if the value is null?
!! forces access and throws NullPointerException if the value is null.
Which operator provides an alternative value if something is null?
\?: is the Elvis operator and allows returning a default value.
Which keyword is used to declare a class?
class declares a class in Kotlin.
Which keyword creates a singleton in Kotlin?
Which modifier allows a class to be inherited in Kotlin?
Classes are final by default. To allow inheritance, open is used.
Which keyword is used to override a method?
override indicates that a member from a parent class or interface is being overridden.
Which keyword calls the parent class implementation?
super allows access to the superclass implementation.
Which keyword refers to the current object?
this refers to the current instance.
Which structure is used to select between several cases?
Kotlin uses when instead of switch.
Which function prints text with a line break?
println() prints text and adds a line break.
Which function prints text without a line break?
print() prints text without an automatic line break.
Which type represents double-precision decimal numbers?
Which type represents a single character?
How do you normally define an immutable list in Kotlin?
How do you normally define a mutable list in Kotlin?
Which function creates a read-only map?
Which function creates a read-only set?
Which function transforms each element of a collection?
map transforms each element and returns a new collection.
Which function filters elements from a collection?
filter returns the elements that satisfy a condition.
Which function executes an action for each element?
forEach executes an action for each element.
Which keyword is used to create a data class?
data class automatically generates useful functions such as equals, hashCode and toString.
Which scope function returns the result of the lambda?
let executes a lambda and returns its result.
Which scope function returns the object itself after configuring properties?
apply returns the receiver object, useful for configuring properties.
What is the name of the implicit parameter in many single-parameter lambdas?
it is the implicit parameter name if no other name is declared.
interface declares an interface in Kotlin.
Which keyword or symbol is used to declare class inheritance or interface implementation?
Kotlin uses the colon : for inheritance and implementation.
Which modifier makes a class unable to be instantiated directly?
abstract means that the class cannot be instantiated directly.
Which special type represents the absence of a useful return value?
Unit is similar to void in other languages.
Which type indicates that a function never returns normally?
Nothing represents a function that does not return normally, for example because it throws an exception.
Which keyword is used for packages?
package defines the package that a Kotlin file belongs to.
Which keyword imports classes or functions?
import allows using classes or functions from other packages.
Which keyword is used to control visibility only inside the file?
At top level, private limits visibility to the file.
Which modifier limits visibility to the module?
internal makes an element visible inside the same module.
Which function converts a string to a nullable integer if conversion fails?
toIntOrNull() returns null if the conversion fails.
Which function joins collection elements into a String?
joinToString() concatenates elements into a string.
Which keyword is used to create a loop over a collection?
for iterates over ranges or collections.
Which expression creates a range from 1 to 5 including both ends?
1..5 creates a closed range from 1 to 5.
Which expression creates a range from 1 up to but not including 5?
Which word is used to iterate downward?
Which word allows skipping values in a range?
step defines the jump between values in a progression.
In Kotlin, val declares a read-only reference.
val prevents reassigning the reference after initialization.
In Kotlin, var declares a mutable variable.
var allows changing the value of the variable.
Kotlin uses fun to declare functions.
Kotlin requires a semicolon at the end of every line.
In Kotlin, semicolons are normally not necessary.
String represents text strings in Kotlin.
Int stores whole numbers, not decimals.
A String? type can contain null.
A String type can always contain null even without ?.
Without ?, String does not accept null.
The ?. operator avoids accessing a property if the object is null.
The !! operator is completely safe and never fails.
!! can throw NullPointerException if the value is null.
The ?: operator allows giving a default value if something is null.
Kotlin uses when as a powerful alternative to switch.
Kotlin has the switch keyword just like Java.
Kotlin classes are final by default.
To allow inheritance, open is used.
open allows a class to be inherited.
open opens a class or method for inheritance or overriding.
override is used to override members.
data class automatically generates equals and toString.
Data classes automatically generate useful functions.
mutableListOf() allows adding and removing elements.
map transforms elements of a collection.
filter keeps the elements that satisfy a condition.
forEach executes an action for each element.
forEach iterates over elements and executes a lambda.
it can be the implicit parameter name in lambdas.
Kotlin uses it when there is a single implicit parameter.
apply returns the receiver object itself.
apply is useful for configuring objects.
let always returns the original object itself.
let returns the result of the lambda.
Unit indicates the absence of a useful return value.
Nothing represents a function that does not return normally.
Nothing is used, for example, in functions that throw an exception.
Kotlin uses : for inheritance and implementation.
The : symbol is used in both cases.
abstract allows declaring classes that cannot be instantiated directly.
An abstract class is not instantiated directly.
package defines the package of a Kotlin file.
package indicates the logical location of the code.
import allows using elements from other packages.
import brings classes, functions or objects into the file.
internal limits visibility to the module.
internal allows access inside the same module.
Top-level private limits visibility to the file.
Top-level private remains visible only in the file.
toIntOrNull() returns null if it cannot convert.
toIntOrNull avoids an exception and returns null if conversion fails.
joinToString() joins elements into a string.
joinToString converts a collection into joined text.
The closed range 1..5 includes both ends.
step allows specifying jumps in a range.
step defines the increment or jump.
println() prints with a line break.
print() prints without an automatic line break.
print does not add a line break.
== and === mean exactly the same thing in Kotlin.
== compares structural equality and === compares referential equality.
val prevents modifying the internal content of a mutable object.
val prevents reassigning the reference, but the object can be mutable.
var always means that the internal object is mutable.
var allows reassigning the reference, but it does not guarantee internal mutability.
var allows changing the value of the variable.
String values are written between double quotes.
Complete the code:\n\nval name: String____ = null
\? allows the type to accept null.
\?. accesses only if name is not null.
Complete the code:\n\nval finalName = name ____ "No name"
\?: returns the alternative value if name is null.
class Person declares a class named Person.
open allows inheriting from the class.
The child class inherits using : Animal().
super allows calling the parent implementation.
this refers to the current object.
Kotlin uses when for multiple branches.
The if block is written between braces.
step defines the jump between values.
add adds an element to a mutable list.
Complete the code:\n\nval squares = numbers.____ { it * it }
Complete the code:\n\nval evens = numbers.____ { it % 2 == 0 }
filter keeps the elements that satisfy the condition.
forEach executes an action for each element.
Complete the code:\n\n____ class User(val name: String)
data class automatically generates useful functions.
Complete the code:\n\nval text = name.____ { uppercase() }
let executes a lambda and returns its result.
Complete the code:\n\nval person = Person().____ { name = "Ana" }
apply configures the object and returns the same object.
it is the implicit lambda parameter.
Complete the code:\n\n____ Flyable { fun fly() }
Kotlin uses : to implement interfaces or inherit.
abstract declares a class that cannot be instantiated directly.
Unit means there is no useful return value.
Complete the code:\n\nfun fail(): ____ { throw Exception() }
Nothing represents a function that does not return normally.
import allows using elements from other packages.
Top-level private limits visibility to the file.
internal limits visibility to the module.
toIntOrNull returns null if the conversion fails.
joinToString joins elements into a string.
while executes a block between braces.
return returns an expression or value.
val declares a read-only reference that cannot be reassigned.
var allows reassigning the value of a variable.
Match the concept: safe call operator
Accesses only if the object is not null
The ?. operator allows accessing properties or methods only if the value is not null.
Returns an alternative value if something is null
The ?: operator returns a default value when the expression on the left is null.
Class that automatically generates equals, hashCode and toString
Class that can only have abstract methods
Class that represents a SQL table
A data class automatically generates useful functions for data models.
internal limits visibility to the current module.
A variable declared with val is being reassigned
The required String type is missing
Kotlin does not allow local variables
val does not allow reassigning the reference. To change the value, var must be used.
A String is being assigned to an Int variable
Int must be written in lowercase
"25" is text. For Int, 25 must be used without quotes.
Find the error:\n\nval name: String = null
String does not accept null unless it has ?
The String type does not exist in Kotlin
To accept null, the type should be String?.
Lists do not exist in Kotlin
listOf() creates a read-only list. To add elements, mutableListOf() is used.
Animal must be open or abstract to be inherited
In Kotlin, classes are final by default. The parent class must be open or abstract.

Find the error:

val text: String? = null
println(text.length)
text may be null and length cannot be accessed directly
length does not exist on String
String? does not exist in Kotlin
If text is nullable, text?.length should be used, or null should be checked first.

What does this code print?

val x = 5
println(x + 2)

What does this code print?

val name = "Isra"
println("Hello $name")
Kotlin allows variable interpolation in Strings using $name.

What does this code print?

val numbers = listOf(1, 2, 3)
println(numbers.map { it * 2 })
map transforms each element by multiplying it by 2.

What does this code print?

val name: String? = null
println(name ?: "No name")
The Elvis operator ?: returns the alternative value if name is null.

What does this code print?

val text = "25"
println(text.toIntOrNull())
toIntOrNull() correctly converts "25" to an integer.
Order the lines to declare a function that greets:
First the function is declared, then the body, and finally the closing brace.
Order the lines to create a list and iterate over it:
First the list is created, then it is iterated with forEach, and each element is printed.
Order the lines to use a condition:
The if opens its block, then comes the else, and finally the block is closed.
Order the lines to transform a list:
First the list is created, then it is transformed, and finally the result is printed.
Guess the term: keyword used to declare functions.
In Kotlin, functions are declared with fun.
Guess the term: structure that replaces switch.
Kotlin uses when to handle several branches.
Guess the term: operator that forces a nullable value.
!! forces access and can throw NullPointerException if the value is null.
Guess the term: modifier that allows inheriting from a class.
open allows a class or method to be inherited or overridden.
Guess the term: visibility limited to the module.
internal makes an element visible inside the same module.
Clue: keyword used to declare a mutable variable.
Clue: keyword used to declare a class.
class declares a class in Kotlin.
Clue: type that represents the absence of a useful value.
Unit represents the absence of a useful return value.
Clue: function that transforms each element of a collection.
map transforms each element and returns a new collection.
Clue: type that represents a function that never returns normally.
Nothing represents functions that do not return normally, for example because they throw an exception.
